Title: Regulation of growth–defense balance by the JASMONATE ZIM-DOMAIN (JAZ)-MYC transcriptional module

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JASMONATE ZIM-DOMAIN (JAZ) proteins; TIFY family repressors with ZIM and Jas domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites involved in resistance to insect herbivory and specialized metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves for anthocyanin and glucosinolate-related defense traits; roots for marneral/thalianol triterpenoid cluster expression
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of JAZ1/3/4/9/10 constitutively activates jasmonate responses, increasing anti-insect defense and expression of glucosinolate biosynthetic/catabolic and triterpenoid biosynthetic genes, while restricting leaf growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order loss-of-function mutant analysis (jazQ); genetic epistasis with aos, coi1 and myc2 myc3 myc4; insect feeding assay; pathogen assay; chlorophyll assay; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in seedlings, roots, rosette leaves, flowers and inflorescences under standard growth conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous methyl jasmonate; darkness during chlorophyll degradation assay; insect herbivory by Trichoplusia ni; infection by Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core study focus is the JAZ-repressible signaling module; JAZ depletion up-regulates glucosinolate biosynthetic genes in a MYC-dependent manner but glucosinolate catabolic genes and triterpenoid cluster genes in a MYC-independent manner; JAZ3 may positively regulate root growth under low-JA conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of JAZ1/3/4/9/10 causes constitutive jasmonate signaling and growth–defense reprogramming. 2. JAZ depletion enhances defense metabolite-associated transcription, including glucosinolate metabolism and triterpenoid cluster genes. 3. Distinct JAZ-controlled outputs are partitioned into MYC-dependent and MYC-independent sectors.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced leaf biomass and area; delayed flowering; increased susceptibility to Pst DC3000 despite stronger anti-insect defense

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JA co-receptor; F-box protein of SCFCOI1 complex
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of COI1 suppresses constitutive growth–defense antagonism of jazQ, showing JAZ depletion effects depend on jasmonate perception
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic epistasis via jazQ coi1 sextuple mutant; root growth inhibition assay; fertility phenotyping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core signaling component experimentally tested in Results, but not a pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator for a specific nutrient metabolite
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1 is epistatic to jazQ for leaf growth and fertility traits. 2. coi1 abolishes jazQ root hypersensitivity to exogenous jasmonate. 3. Intact JA perception is required for constitutive JAZ-depletion phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Male sterility; loss of jasmonate responsiveness

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AOS suppresses constitutive shoot growth–defense antagonism of jazQ, indicating JAZ-depletion phenotypes require JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic epistasis via jazQ aos sextuple mutant; root growth assay; fertility phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOS is upstream in JA biosynthesis and was assayed as a causal pathway component required for constitutive jasmonate-driven defense traits rather than as a direct enzyme for the terminal nutrient metabolite itself
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aos suppresses jazQ leaf growth and fertility phenotypes. 2. JA biosynthesis is required for constitutive JAZ-depletion outputs. 3. aos does not rescue the constitutive short-root phenotype of jazQ.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Male sterility and impaired jasmonate-mediated defense signaling

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: MYC2/jin1-7: SALK_040500; MYC3: GK-445B11; MYC4: GK-491E10
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivory and specialized metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of jasmonate-responsive defense metabolites and broader growth–defense transcriptional reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of jasmonate-responsive metabolic genes and repression of growth-associated processes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates JA biosynthesis and glucosinolate biosynthetic genes; required for expression of wound-response genes such as VSP2 and TAT1; not required for induction of triterpenoid cluster genes or glucosinolate catabolic genes in jazQ
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LOX2; BCAT4; IPMD1; CYP79B3; TAT1; VSP2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling relieved from JAZ repression; exogenous methyl jasmonate
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Supports positive feedforward amplification of JA responses through induction of JA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulators of the major MYC-dependent sector of JAZ-repressible defense metabolism, particularly glucosinolate biosynthesis and anti-insect defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYC2/3/4 are required for jazQ-enhanced anti-insect defense, JA hypersensitivity, chlorophyll degradation, elevated susceptibility to Pst DC3000, and much of glucosinolate-related transcription; they positively regulate anthocyanin but are not solely responsible for its accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant analysis (mycT) and jazQ mycT octuple mutant; root growth inhibition assays; chlorophyll degradation assay; insect feeding assay; pathogen infection assay; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in seedlings and leaves under basal conditions and after MeJA-related phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous methyl jasmonate; darkness; insect herbivory; bacterial infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Act in the JAZ-MYC transcriptional module; JAZ repressors interact with and inhibit MYCs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYC2/3/4 are not required for delayed flowering of jazQ and do not mediate MYC-independent induction of triterpenoid cluster genes or glucosinolate catabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2/3/4 mediate the enhanced responsiveness of jazQ to exogenous JA. 2. Loss of MYC2/3/4 fully rescues jazQ leaf growth repression and enhanced anti-insect defense. 3. MYCs control a major but not complete fraction of JAZ-repressible metabolic gene expression, especially glucosinolate biosynthesis, while triterpenoid cluster and glucosinolate catabolic genes remain MYC-independent.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promote growth repression, increased susceptibility to Pst DC3000, and chlorophyll degradation under JA signaling; loss of MYCs weakens anti-insect defense
